sanskrit: सत्त्वानुरूपा सर्वस्य श्रद्धा भवति भारत । श्रद्धामयोऽयं पुरुषो यो यच्छ्रद्धः स एव सः ॥
hindiTranslation: हे भारत  सभी मनुष्योंकी श्रद्धा अन्तःकरणके अनुरूप होती है ।  यह मनुष्य श्रद्धामय है ।  इसलिये जो जैसी श्रद्धावाला है, वही उसका स्वरूप है अर्थात् वही उसकी निष्ठा  स्थिति है ॥
englishTranslation: O Bharata, each person's trust matches their inner nature. A person is made of trust; whatever trust he holds, that is what he becomes.
essence: Trust is not just held; it becomes the person.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person's deepest trust reflects their inner makeup, and that trust shapes who they become.
simpleInsight: What you repeatedly trust quietly forms your character.
detailedMeaning: In the middle of Arjuna's crisis on the battlefield, Krishna explains that faith is not random. Each person's trust grows from their inner condition, and that trust then shapes their whole life and identity.
modernRelevance: You keep returning to the same app, the same voice, the same habit, the same fear. What you trust most quietly starts to define you.
translationLiteral: सत्त्वानुरूपा (according to inner nature) / सर्वस्य (of everyone) / श्रद्धा (trust before certainty) / भवति (becomes) / भारत (O Bharata) । / श्रद्धामयः (made of trust) / अयम् (this) / पुरुषः (person) / यः (who) / यत् (whatever) / श्रद्धः (trustful) / सः (that) / एव (indeed) / सः (he) ॥
insight: It shifts attention from surface belief to the deeper loyalties that form character and action.

quoteVariants.text: Your trust shapes you. You become what you hold sacred.
Trust shapes the one who trusts.
I become what I keep trusting.
